pageCaption.value: When the explorers at Under The Pole dive into the Arctic Ocean, they face formidable challenges. The water is so cold it hurts, and the chill also wears out their equipment. Arctic dives require careful preparation and the right frame of mind but the reward is discovery. The team has found the first-ever marine animal forest in the Arctic off the coast of Svalbard, as part of its ongoing DEEPLIFE programme supported by the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative.

WaPageTitle.value: Under The Pole: DEEPLIFE - Svalbard Expedition - Episode 2

metadescription.value: Researchers are collecting novel data about the unexplored marine animal forests of the Arctic Ocean for future study. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Under The Pole: DEEPLIFE - Svalbard Expedition - Episode 2

title.value: Under The Pole: DEEPLIFE - Svalbard Expedition - Episode 2

text.value: When the explorers at Under The Pole dive into the Arctic Ocean, they face formidable challenges. The water is so cold it hurts, and the chill also wears out their equipment. Arctic dives require careful preparation and the right frame of mind but the reward is discovery. The team has found the first-ever marine animal forest in the Arctic off the coast of Svalbard, as part of its ongoing DEEPLIFE programme supported by the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative.

pageCaption.value: Vreni Häusserman is a German biologist who is exploring the unique marine life of the Patagonian fjords in the south of Chile. These extraordinary and delicate underwater environments are suffering from the effects of intensive fish farming and global warming. Vreni is planning ever-deeper dives to study, understand and preserve this unique biodiversity before it is too late.

metadescription.value: Vreni Häusserman is a German biologist who is exploring the unique marine life of the Patagonian fjords in the south of Chile. These extraordinary and delicate underwater environments are suffering from the effects of intensive fish farming and global warming. pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 1

pageCaption.value: “Think like an ocean”. Working to protect the iconic Galápagos Islands Marine Reserve, Sylvia Earle and her Mission Blue team led a groundbreaking expedition to assess the health of the ocean after nearly 25 years of protection. What they found could be used as a blueprint of success for the rest of the planet.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 1

metadescription.value: 25 years after the creation of the Galápagos Marine Reserve, an expedition has been launched to collect new data. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 2

pageCaption.value: Mission Blue has been helping to track animals in the Galápagos Marine Reserve to analyse their well-being around the archipelago. Through this work, legendary oceanographer Sylvia Earle and a team of scientists are making incredible insights into the connectivity of the Galápagos with the rest of the planet – including discovering DNA sequences that could be completely new to science.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 2

metadescription.value: Using eDNA, researchers are aiming to characterize the marine diversity of the Galápagos to protect specific habitats. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageCaption.value: By exploring the Arctic seas, the team at Under The Pole aims to document the region’s hidden ecosystems and preserve them. Only by understanding the marine animal communities that exist in these icy waters – and how they are changing – is it possible to take action. Divers Ghislain Bardout and Emmanuelle Périé-Bardout want to ensure a living world for the next generation. Their science-driven exploration is supported by the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative.

WaPageTitle.value: Under The Pole: DEEPLIFE - Svalbard Expedition - Episode 3

metadescription.value: By exploring marine animal forests in the Arctic, scientists aim to safeguard the environment for future generations. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Under The Pole: DEEPLIFE - Svalbard Expedition - Episode 3

pageCaption.value: The ecosystems of the Arctic seas remain mysterious even as the sea ice retreats due to climate change. To learn more, Under The Pole’s Ghislain Bardout and Emmanuelle Périé-Bardout led an expedition to Svalbard, supported by the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative. The explorers dived into the frigid waters in search of marine animal forests. These ecosystems are just as essential to life on Earth as forests on land, yet little is known about them.

WaPageTitle.value: Under The Pole: DEEPLIFE - Svalbard Expedition - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Through the DEEPLIFE programme, researchers have set out to study the Arctic Ocean's unexplored marine animal forests. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Under The Pole: DEEPLIFE - Svalbard Expedition - Episode 1

pageTitle.value: Deepsea Under The Pole by Rolex (Extended) - Video

pageCaption.value: Deepsea Under The Pole by Rolex is a pioneering expedition and innovative human adventure that combines ski trekking and scuba diving in one of the toughest climates on the planet. Throughout the journey, the eight expedition members collected audio recordings, photographs and video material of the hidden side of the polar ice cap, in addition to successfully conducting two scientific experiments related to snow measurements and human body reactions.
